methods.description: This method involves estimating the spectral characteristics of proxy records using a fast Fourier transform algorithm and evaluating significant frequencies shared between proxy reconstructions and key climatic indices using nonparametric coherence multitaper method and phase analysis.

steps.description: The spectral characteristics of the proxy records were estimated using a simple fast Fourier transform algorithm.
steps.searchable_summary: perform fast Fourier transform on proxy records

steps.description: Significance level (90%) was estimated using 10,000 Monte Carlo simulations.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate significance level using Monte Carlo simulations

steps.description: Nonparametric coherence multitaper method and phase analysis with bias correction were performed to evaluate the significant frequencies shared between proxy reconstructions and key climatic indices.
steps.searchable_summary: perform multitaper coherence method and phase analysis

steps.description: Spectral coherence was estimated with 8 windows and 50 iterations on Monte Carlo error estimates.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate spectral coherence with specified parameters

steps.description: A Gaussian filter centered at significant frequencies was applied using the Analyseries software to further isolate and observe specific frequencies through time.
steps.searchable_summary: apply Gaussian filter to isolate frequencies

methods.description: This method describes the process of measuring Sr/Ca and δ18O in coral samples using spectrometry and isotope ratio mass spectrometry, including calibration and correction for instrument drift and matrix effects.

steps.description: Sr/Ca subsamples were dissolved in 2.5 ml of 5% HNO3 overnight.
An inductively coupled plasma-optical emission spectrometry was used to measure Sr and Ca simultaneously.
steps.searchable_summary: measure Sr and Ca using ICP-OES

steps.description: Instrument drift and matrix effects were corrected using solution standards run at constant intervals.
steps.searchable_summary: correct for instrument drift and matrix effects

steps.description: Oxygen stable isotopes, δ18O, were measured in an automated Kiel IV carbonate device coupled with a ThermoFisher MAT-253 isotope ratio mass spectrometer.
steps.searchable_summary: measure δ18O using isotope ratio mass spectrometer

steps.description: Measurements were calibrated relative to Vienna Peedee belemnite using external standards.
steps.searchable_summary: calibrate δ18O measurements using external standards

methods.description: This method describes the collection of a coral core from a specific location using an underwater hydraulic drill, including details about the study site and environmental conditions.

steps.description: A Porites sp. coral core was collected from 6-m water depth off the coast of Houbihu, southern Taiwan.
steps.searchable_summary: collect coral core using underwater hydraulic drill

steps.description: The 3-m-long core was drilled using an underwater hydraulic drill with an~85-mm diameter core bit.
steps.searchable_summary: drill coral core with specified equipment

methods.description: This method involves collecting and selecting instrumental data for sea surface temperature (SST) and salinity from various datasets for calibration and comparison purposes.

steps.description: Monthly 1°by 1°grid resolution SST data from the Integrated Global Ocean Services System Products Bulletin was used for SST calibration over a 31-year period.
steps.searchable_summary: fetch SST data from IGOSS for calibration

steps.description: Monthly rainfall data from 1897 to 2013 from a local weather office in Hengchun County was used for comparison.
steps.searchable_summary: fetch rainfall data from local weather office
